text.title: Market Analysis Common Mistakes and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Common mistakes in market analysis and agent-specific failure modes with mitigation rules.

antipattern.description: Running only top-down sizing without a bottom-up cross-check
antipattern.reason: Top-down numbers from analyst reports are the ceiling, not the plan. Without a bottom-up check, the SOM is detached from operational reality and cannot be defended to investors or the team.

antipattern.description: Feature-matrix based on live pages without a snapshot
antipattern.reason: A live-page feature matrix cannot be reproduced 6 months later, cannot be diffed to show competitive movement, and is immediately stale as competitors ship.

antipattern.description: Declaring whitespace without review-site evidence of buyer demand
antipattern.reason: Feature gaps can be intentional. Without buyer demand evidence, a whitespace claim leads to building features nobody needs while ignoring the actual gaps buyers care about.

antipattern.description: Using a competitor list that omits indirect and substitute competitors
antipattern.reason: Indirect and substitute competitors are the most common source of disruption. A list of only direct competitors produces a false sense of the competitive moat.

antipattern.description: Agent conflates "market size" (revenue) with "user count" and reports them interchangeably
antipattern.reason: SAM in dollars and SAM in seats are different artifacts with different implications for pricing and GTM. Always demand explicit units in every market-size assertion.

antipattern.description: Agent cites CAGR with two decimal places without a source
antipattern.reason: Treat any CAGR more precise than the nearest 5% as fabricated unless it has a source URL and a page number. Real analyst reports round to one decimal place; invented numbers have false precision.

antipattern.description: Agent silently truncates pricing tables from long SaaS landing pages
antipattern.reason: Web-fetch tools cut off page content; pricing tables at the bottom of long pages are omitted. Pass --only-main-content and verify that the output includes the full pricing section.

antipattern.description: Agent cites Gartner or Forrester reports it cannot access because they are paywalled
antipattern.reason: An agent that cannot access a source will sometimes return a confident summary anyway. Require a public URL or mark the source as "(source unverified)"; never accept a paywalled citation without the original text.

antipattern.description: Agent's feature-matrix pass degrades after 10 competitors in long context
antipattern.reason: Chunk the matrix to 5 competitors per invocation and merge the JSON. Long-context degradation causes missed features and incorrect Y/N/P assignments past the 10-competitor mark.

antipattern.description: Agent marks a single-channel SAM as "100% reachable" without a CAC ceiling
antipattern.reason: Every channel has a ceiling — the total reachable accounts via that channel at a given CAC. Without a ceiling, the GTM plan exceeds the actual addressable population.
